page: Amazon Web Services m6gd.8xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by 32 dedicated physical cores of the ARMv8 AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz. The arm64 architecture features 1.0 thread per core, 64 KB L1D cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of memory, providing 4.0 GB per core, and includes a bundled 1900 GB NVMe SSD local instance store. Network connectivity is delivered via a 12 Gbps baseline bandwidth on the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark results show average multi-core and memory performance. The server achieved a multi-core stress-ng CPU score of 48563.19 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 11587.0. Memory bandwidth tests recorded 1894.51 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 166.08 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. However, single-core performance is weaker, with a Geekbench single-core score of 950.0 and a single-threaded gzip compression rate of 20.31 GB/sec. Cryptographic operations are supported via AES, yielding a Passmark CPU encryption score of 4374.37.

This server is designed for workloads that benefit from local NVMe storage and balanced compute-to-memory ratios, such as database operations, static web serving, and multi-threaded decompression. Passmark database operations reached 20452.11, and static web serving throughput was measured at 33.39 GB/sec. While multi-threaded bzip3 decompression performs at an average level of 61.18 GB/sec, single-threaded decompression is poor at 7.38 GB/sec. Additionally, LLM text generation is limited, with the 135M model achieving 39.3 tokens/sec.
description: Amazon Web Services m6gd.8xlarge is a general-purpose server featuring 32 dedicated ARMv8 AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 physical cores running at 2.5 GHz. Built on the arm64 architecture and managed by the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 128.0 GB of memory and a bundled 1900 GB NVMe SSD local instance store. The instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. Performance benchmarks indicate average multi-core capabilities, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 11587.0 and a multi-core stress-ng CPU score of 48563.19, though single-core performance is weaker. The server is suited for database operations, static web serving, and multi-threaded applications, supported by a Passmark database score of 20452.11 and static web serving throughput of 33.39 GB/sec.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6gd.8xlarge is an arm64 general-purpose server with 32 Graviton cores, 128 GB RAM, and a 1900 GB NVMe SSD, delivering balanced multi-core performance and 12 Gbps network baseline.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6gd.8xlarge is an arm64 server with 32 Graviton cores, 128 GB RAM, and 1900 GB NVMe SSD, optimized for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: An arm64 general-purpose server featuring dedicated Graviton cores, balanced memory, and high-capacity local NVMe SSD storage for multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 32 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 ARMv8 cores at 2.5 GHz.
Includes 128.0 GB of system memory and a 1900 GB NVMe SSD local instance store.
Delivers a 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via the AWS Nitro hypervisor.
Demonstrates average multi-core performance with a Geekbench multi-core score of 11587.0.
Well-suited for database operations, static web serving, and software compilation.
